fields.description: Disable to stop sending and receiving emails form this cluster.
fields.label: Enabled

fields.description: MX record priority for handling inbound mail. Lower values have higher priority.
fields.label: Priority

fields.description: Key for authenticating HTTP requests. Generate one via Actions > Generate API Key.
fields.label: API Key

fields.description: URL where HTTP requests are sent before being forwarded to mail servers.
fields.label: Base URL

fields.description: Manage data, blob, full-text, and lookup stores.
fields.label: Stores

fields.description: Public IPv4 addresses assigned to this cluster.
fields.label: IPv4 Addresses

fields.description: Public IPv6 addresses assigned to this cluster.
fields.label: IPv6 Addresses

fields.description: Manage SMTP, IMAP, HTTP, and other listeners.
fields.label: Listeners

fields.description: Stores user accounts, passwords, email addresses, and settings. Used for authentication, email validation, and account management.
fields.label: Directory Storage

fields.description: Core storage unit where email metadata, folders, and various settings are stored. Essentially, it contains all the data except for large binary objects (blobs).
fields.label: Data Storage

fields.description: Used for storing large binary objects such as emails, sieve scripts, and other files.
fields.label: Blob Storage

fields.description: Dedicated to indexing for full-text search, enhancing the speed and efficiency of text-based queries.
fields.label: Full Text Index Storage

fields.description: Key-value storage used primarily by the SMTP server and anti-spam components.
fields.label: In-Memory Storage

fields.description: The UDP port the gossip protocol will bind to.
fields.label: Bind Port

fields.description: Make this cluster available for selection when creating a new tenant.
fields.label: Public

fields.description: Time to wait between retry attempts.
fields.label: Retry Interval (Milliseconds)

fields.description: Total number of push subscriptions that a given user can activate.
fields.label: Max Subscriptions

fields.description: Time to wait before sending a new request to the push service.
fields.label: Throttle (Milliseconds)

fields.description: Maximum number of push attempts before a notification is discarded.
fields.label: Max Attempts

fields.description: Time to wait between push attempts.
fields.label: Attempt Interval (Milliseconds)

fields.description: Time before a connection with a push service URL times out.
fields.label: Request Timeout (Milliseconds)

fields.description: Time to wait for the push service to verify a subscription.
fields.label: Verification Timeout (Milliseconds)

fields.description: The key used to encrypt gossip messages.
fields.label: Encryption Key

fields.description: Enable proxy protocol for connections from these networks.
fields.label: Trusted Networks

fields.description: Default language to use when language detection is not possible.
fields.label: Default Language

fields.description: Username for administrative access to the cluster.
fields.label: Username

fields.description: Password for administrative access to the cluster.
fields.label: Password

fields.description: FQDN of the proxy or load balancer forwarding requests to mail servers.
fields.label: Hostname

fields.description: Restricts the number of concurrent requests a user can make to the JMAP server.
fields.label: Concurrent

fields.description: Defines the maximum size of a single request, in bytes, that the server will accept.
fields.label: Size (Bytes)

fields.description: Limits the maximum number of method calls that can be included in a single request.
fields.label: Method Calls

fields.description: Determines the maximum number of objects that can be fetched in a single method call.
fields.label: Get

fields.description: Establishes the maximum number of objects that can be modified in a single method call.
fields.label: Set

fields.description: Sets the maximum number of results that a Query method can return.
fields.label: Query

fields.description: Determines the maximum number of change objects that a Changes method can return.
fields.label: Changes

fields.description: Defines the maximum file size for file uploads to the server.
fields.label: Max Size (Bytes)

fields.description: Restricts the number of concurrent file uploads a user can perform.
fields.label: Max Concurrent

fields.description: Specifies the Time-To-Live (TTL) for each uploaded file, after which the file is deleted from temporary storage.
fields.label: Expire After (Hours)

fields.description: Specifies the maximum number of files that a user can upload within a certain period.
fields.label: Total Files

fields.description: Defines the total size of files that a user can upload within a certain period.
fields.label: Total Size (Bytes)

fields.description: Restricts the maximum depth of nested mailboxes a user can create.
fields.label: Max Depth

fields.description: Establishes the maximum length of a mailbox name.
fields.label: Name Length

fields.description: Specifies the maximum size for an email attachment.
fields.label: Attachment Size (Bytes)

fields.description: Determines the maximum size for an email message.
fields.label: E-mail Size (Bytes)

fields.description: Limits the maximum number of items that can be parsed from an email message.
fields.label: Parse Max

fields.description:
Specifies how often tombstoned messages are deleted from the database. 

*  *  *  *  *
┬  ┬  ┬  ┬  ┬
│  │  │  │  │
│  │  │  │  └ day of week (0 - 6) (0 is Sunday)
│  │  │  └───── month (1 - 12)
│  │  └────────── day of month (1 - 31)
│  └─────────────── hour (0 - 23)
└──────────────────── minute (0 - 59)

---

* - Any value
/ - Step values
fields.label: Frequency (Cron)

fields.description: How long to keep messages in the Trash and Junk Mail folders before auto-expunging.
fields.label: Trash Auto-Expunge (Days)

fields.description: How many changes to keep in the history for each account. This is used to determine the changes that have occurred since the last time the client requested changes.
fields.label: Changes History

fields.description: Allow users to configure encryption at rest for their data.
fields.label: Enable encryption at rest

fields.description: Encrypt messages that are manually appended by the user using JMAP or IMAP.
fields.label: Encrypt on append

fields.description: Enable the Prometheus metrics endpoint.
fields.label: Enable

fields.description: The Prometheus endpoint's username for Basic authentication.
fields.label: Username

fields.description: The Prometheus endpoint's secret for Basic authentication.
fields.label: Secret

fields.description: The transport protocol for Open Telemetry.
fields.label: Transport

fields.description: The endpoint for Open Telemetry.
fields.label: Endpoint

fields.description: Maximum amount of time that Stalwart will wait for a response from the OpenTelemetry endpoint.
fields.label: Timeout (Seconds)

fields.description: The minimum amount of time that must pass between each push request to the OpenTelemetry endpoint.
fields.label: Push Interval (Seconds)

fields.description: Manage logging and tracing methods.
fields.label: Traces
